title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the King's Head Inn, Kitcher questions Mr. Mick regarding how a gentleman discovered Kitcher's identity as a London policeman. Suspecting Mick is lying, Kitcher moves to sit with an elderly man named Walter Stückri, who has a wine-soaked goat's beard and a garlic-scented nose. Through a disjointed conversation about success and failure, Stückri discusses the cases of Harriet Sippele, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nix.

Stückri reveals that Harriet Sippele's life was shaped by a "forgivable lie" and the loss of her husband, George, who died young of a disease. He notes that Harriet was approximately forty-two or forty-three years old and was deeply devoted to a wealthy man named Patrick. Kitcher deduces that Harriet was twenty years older than a local loafer and had chosen the successful Patrick over a different path. Kitcher attempts to confirm if Patrick's surname initials are PJI and if there is a woman named Jenny in Glebeholme, but Stückri's rambling logic makes confirmation difficult.

After leaving the inn, Kitcher walks through the quiet, drizzly countryside. He hears passersby mention "police" and "Harriet," leading him to wonder if Victor Muggleton has been spreading news of his presence in the village. While walking along an "S" shaped path, Kitcher encounters a young man with glasses and freckles, whom he had previously met upon his arrival by train.
